title: Snapshots Object
description: Provides access to the Snapshots within a design and provides methods to create new Snapshots.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new snapshot. Creating a snapshot is only valid when the HasPendingTransforms property returns true.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified snapshot in the collection using an index into the collection.

methods.name: revertPendingSnapshot
methods.description: Reverts and changes that have been made that can be snapshot. This effectively reverts the design back to the last snapshot. This is only valid when the HasPendingSnapshot property returns true.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of items in the collection.

properties.name: hasPendingSnapshot
properties.description: Indicates if there are any changes that have been made than can be snapshot.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: Snapshots.add Method
add.description: Creates a new snapshot. Creating a snapshot is only valid when the HasPendingTransforms property returns true.

classType.title: Snapshots.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: Snapshots.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified snapshot in the collection using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

revertPendingSnapshot.title: Snapshots.revertPendingSnapshot Method
revertPendingSnapshot.description: Reverts and changes that have been made that can be snapshot. This effectively reverts the design back to the last snapshot. This is only valid when the HasPendingSnapshot property returns true.
revertPendingSnapshot.return_description: Returns true if the revert was successful.

count.title: Snapshots.count Property
count.description: The number of items in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = snapshots_var.count

hasPendingSnapshot.title: Snapshots.hasPendingSnapshot Property
hasPendingSnapshot.description: Indicates if there are any changes that have been made than can be snapshot.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = snapshots_var.hasPendingSnapshot

isValid.title: Snapshots.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = snapshots_var.isValid

objectType.title: Snapshots.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = snapshots_var.objectType
